subject: Databases
title: Aliases Make Queries Readable
description: Learn Aliases Make Queries Readable in one minute.

hook.text: SELECT u.name, o.total FROM users u JOIN orders o — aliases shorten table names in queries.

buildup.text: Use AS to rename columns in output: SELECT name AS customer_name. Clearer results.

discovery.text: Table aliases are essential in joins. Without them, column names collide: users.id vs orders.id.

twist.text: Aliases exist only in the query. They don't change the table or column in the database.

climax.text: Good aliases make complex queries readable. Bad aliases (a, b, c) make them worse.

punchline.text: Aliases: short but meaningful. Readability matters.

quiz.question: Why are table aliases essential when writing JOIN queries?
options: They prevent column name collisions between tables with identical column names
They make the query run faster
The database requires them for every query
